payload.audience: application developers using dali-ui public APIs

content_quality.default_object_model: Use Dali::Ui::View rather than Dali::Actor for app-facing object examples.
content_quality.guide_assumption: The reader is using dali-ui to build applications.

code_verification: receiver method exists on the receiver type
static methods are called statically and member methods are called on valid instances
assignment target is compatible with the method return type
argument count, argument type, and overload shape match the public API
enum owner and enum value both exist
property constants use the correct owner and value type
signal callback signatures match the public signal typedef
code blocks do not rely on inferred namespaces or overloads from naming alone
draft_requirements: Write enough practical detail for application developers to use the feature in a dali-ui app.
Ground natural-language behavior claims in the actual public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when the context pack is not enough.
Before writing examples, inspect the referenced public headers and relevant sample files when needed.
Prefer examples copied or adapted from existing samples when they match the feature; otherwise keep examples small and verify every API use.
After drafting, review the completed Markdown line by line against repository evidence and fix inaccurate explanations or code before returning.
repair_requirements: Repair only the problematic spans and the smallest surrounding context needed; do not rewrite the whole document.
For each listed issue, use the repository evidence and context pack to verify the corrected wording or code before returning.
If an issue reveals the same API misuse nearby, fix the nearby equivalent misuse too; do not make unrelated style rewrites.
After repair, re-check the edited lines against public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when needed.

rules: Use dali-ui symbols as the app-facing API surface.
Use dali-core and dali-adaptor symbols only as setup/background unless the usage pattern explicitly marks them preferred.
Prefer View-based examples over raw Actor object-model examples.

usage_patterns.intent: React to user or resource events through public signals.
usage_patterns.usage_note: Use signal connection APIs only with their owning trait or class.

rendered_prompt:
You are reviewing an existing DALi dali-ui application developer guide draft.
This is Phase 2.3.5: source-grounded prose review.

Primary goal:
- Improve natural-language accuracy and guide flow using repository evidence.
- Preserve the document's useful structure and examples whenever possible.
- Do not perform a full rewrite.
- Keep the revised user-facing guide in en only.
- Remove Korean, Japanese, Chinese, or other non-English prose from the revised guide.

Feature target:
- Feature: Image (image)
- Category: images-visuals
- Guide profile: dali-ui-app
- Audience: application developers using dali-ui public APIs

Review rules:
- Review every natural-language paragraph line by line against public headers, implementation files, samples, and UTCs when needed.
- Accuracy is the first priority. Flow is the second priority.
- Edit the smallest unit needed: word, sentence, paragraph, or section-local addition/removal.
- If a word-level change is enough, change only the word.
- If a sentence-level change is enough, change only the sentence.
- If an entire paragraph is based on a wrong premise, revise, add, or remove that paragraph.
- Preserve code blocks when possible.
- If prose can be corrected to match an already accurate code block, prefer changing prose.
- If the code block itself makes the prose inaccurate or breaks the guide flow, rewrite only the smallest necessary part of that code block and verify it against public headers/source.
- Do not invent APIs.
- Do not add generic filler just to make the document longer.
- Do not change section structure unless prose_review.allow_section_restructure is true or the current prose is clearly misleading.
- Prefer localized edits when prose_review.localized_edits_only is true.
- Preserve code blocks especially when prose_review.preserve_code_blocks is true.
- Prefer Dali::Ui::View, typed setters, fluent chaining, and view-tree idioms for app-facing guidance.
- Treat dali-core and dali-adaptor APIs as reference/context unless the feature explicitly requires them.

Output contract:
- Put the revised full Markdown between -----BEGIN_REVISED_MARKDOWN----- and -----END_REVISED_MARKDOWN-----.
- Put the concise prose review report between -----BEGIN_PROSE_REVIEW_REPORT----- and -----END_PROSE_REVIEW_REPORT-----.
- Do not include commentary outside those markers.
- Do not wrap the revised Markdown in a code fence.
- Inside the Markdown markers, output the complete final Markdown document only.
- If no prose changes are needed, put the full unchanged original Markdown between the Markdown markers.
- Do not replace the Markdown marker content with status commentary such as 'already valid', 'no changes needed', or 'review complete'.
- The first non-frontmatter content inside the Markdown markers must be the top-level document title heading.
- The review report should list changed prose, source evidence, and remaining concerns.

-----BEGIN_REVISED_MARKDOWN-----
<revised full markdown here>
-----END_REVISED_MARKDOWN-----
-----BEGIN_PROSE_REVIEW_REPORT-----

...
-----END_PROSE_REVIEW_REPORT-----

Original Markdown draft:
---
title: Image
sidebar_label: Image
category: images-visuals
---

# Image

ImageView and AnimatedImageView provide the primary APIs for displaying static and animated images in dali-ui applications.

## Table of Contents

- [Creating an ImageView](#creating-an-imageview)
- [Image Fitting and Sampling](#image-fitting-and-sampling)
- [Loading Policies](#loading-policies)
- [Animated Images](#animated-images)
- [Pixel Area and Sub-Regions](#pixel-area-and-sub-regions)
- [Alpha Masking](#alpha-masking)
- [Resource Signals](#resource-signals)

## Creating an ImageView

Use `ImageView::New()` to create an image view. Pass a URL to load an image from a file path or resource.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/public-api/image-view.h>

using namespace Dali;
using namespace Dali::Ui;

// Create an empty ImageView
ImageView imageView = ImageView::New();

// Create an ImageView with an image URL
ImageView imageView = ImageView::New("images/photo.jpg");

// Set the image URL after creation
imageView.SetResourceUrl("images/another.png");
```

The image loads asynchronously by default. Use `ResourceReadySignal()` to be notified when loading completes.

## Image Fitting and Sampling

Control how the image scales to fit the view bounds using `SetFittingMode()`. The default is `Ui::Image::FittingMode::FILL`, which stretches the image to fill the view.

```cpp
// Preserve aspect ratio (may show empty areas)
imageView.SetFittingMode(Ui::Image::FittingMode::FIT_KEEP_ASPECT_RATIO);

// Stretch to fill the view (may distort)
imageView.SetFittingMode(Ui::Image::FittingMode::FILL);

// Fill while preserving aspect ratio, cropping overflow
imageView.SetFittingMode(Ui::Image::FittingMode::OVER_FIT_KEEP_ASPECT_RATIO);

// Display at original image size
imageView.SetFittingMode(Ui::Image::FittingMode::CENTER);
```

Control the filtering quality when scaling with `SetSamplingMode()`:

```cpp
// Nearest-neighbor sampling (pixelated look)
imageView.SetSamplingMode(Ui::Image::SamplingMode::NEAREST);

// Linear filtering (smooth)
imageView.SetSamplingMode(Ui::Image::SamplingMode::LINEAR);

// High-quality Lanczos filtering
imageView.SetSamplingMode(Ui::Image::SamplingMode::LANCZOS);
```

## Loading Policies

Control when images load and when they release from the texture cache.

### Load Policy

`Ui::Image::LoadPolicy` determines when loading begins:

```cpp
// Load immediately when the view is created
imageView.SetLoadPolicy(Ui::Image::LoadPolicy::IMMEDIATE);

// Defer loading until the view is added to the scene (default)
imageView.SetLoadPolicy(Ui::Image::LoadPolicy::ATTACHED);
```

### Release Policy

`Ui::Image::ReleasePolicy` controls when the texture is released from cache:

```cpp
// Release when the view is removed from the scene
imageView.SetReleasePolicy(Ui::Image::ReleasePolicy::DETACHED);

// Release when the view is destroyed
imageView.SetReleasePolicy(Ui::Image::ReleasePolicy::DESTROYED);

// Never release (keep cached)
imageView.SetReleasePolicy(Ui::Image::ReleasePolicy::NEVER);
```

### Synchronous Loading

For small images where blocking is acceptable, enable synchronous loading:

```cpp
imageView.SetSynchronousLoading(true);
```

### Fast-Track Uploading

Enable background GPU upload to reduce main-thread stalls:

```cpp
imageView.SetFastTrackUpload(true);
```

## Animated Images

`AnimatedImageView` displays animated GIF, WebP, and frame sequences with playback control.

### Basic Playback

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/public-api/animated-image-view.h>

AnimatedImageView animView = AnimatedImageView::New("animations/spinner.gif");

// Start playback
animView.Play();

// Pause playback
animView.Pause();

// Stop and reset to first frame
animView.Stop();
```

### Loop Count

Set how many times the animation repeats:

```cpp
// Loop infinitely
animView.SetLoopCount(-1);

// Loop exactly 3 times
animView.SetLoopCount(3);

// Play once
animView.SetLoopCount(1);
```

### Frame Control

Jump to specific frames and control playback speed:

```cpp
// Jump to frame 5
animView.JumpToFrame(5);

// Get current frame
int currentFrame = animView.GetCurrentFrame();

// Get total frame count
int totalFrames = animView.GetTotalFrame();

// Speed up playback (2x)
animView.SetFrameSpeedFactor(2.0f);

// Slow down playback (0.5x)
animView.SetFrameSpeedFactor(0.5f);
```

### Stop Behavior

Control which frame displays when stopped:

```cpp
// Show current frame when stopped
animView.SetStopBehavior(Ui::AnimatedImage::StopBehavior::CURRENT_FRAME);

// Show first frame when stopped
animView.SetStopBehavior(Ui::AnimatedImage::StopBehavior::FIRST_FRAME);

// Show last frame when stopped
animView.SetStopBehavior(Ui::AnimatedImage::StopBehavior::LAST_FRAME);
```

### Frame Sequence from URLs

Load an animation from individual frame images:

```cpp
Dali::Vector<Dali::String> urls;
urls.PushBack(Dali::String("frames/frame-001.png"));
urls.PushBack(Dali::String("frames/frame-002.png"));
urls.PushBack(Dali::String("frames/frame-003.png"));

animView.SetBatchSize(4);      // Pre-load 4 frames at a time
animView.SetCacheSize(10);     // Cache 10 frames
animView.SetFrameDelay(100);   // 100ms between frames
animView.SetResourceUrls(urls);
animView.Play();
```

## Pixel Area and Sub-Regions

Display a portion of an image using `SetPixelArea()`. The area is specified as normalized coordinates `(x, y, width, height)` in the range `[0, 1]`.

```cpp
// Display the full image
imageView.SetPixelArea(Vector4(0.0f, 0.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f));

// Display the top-left quadrant
imageView.SetPixelArea(Vector4(0.0f, 0.0f, 0.5f, 0.5f));

// Display the center region
imageView.SetPixelArea(Vector4(0.25f, 0.25f, 0.5f, 0.5f));
```

Pixel area works with both `ImageView` and `AnimatedImageView`:

```cpp
AnimatedImageView animView = AnimatedImageView::New("animation.gif");
animView.SetPixelArea(Vector4(0.0f, 0.0f, 0.5f, 0.5f));
animView.Play();
```

## Alpha Masking

Apply an alpha mask to shape the image:

```cpp
// Set the mask image URL
imageView.SetAlphaMaskUrl("masks/rounded-corner-mask.png");

// Crop the image to mask bounds
imageView.SetCropToMask(true);

// Set masking mode
imageView.SetMaskingMode(Ui::Image::MaskingType::MASKING_ON_LOADING);
```

## Resource Signals

Connect to `ResourceReadySignal()` to be notified when an image finishes loading:

```cpp
class MyController : public ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  void SetupImageView()
  {
    mImageView = ImageView::New("images/large-photo.jpg");
    mImageView.ResourceReadySignal().Connect(this, &MyController::OnImageReady);
  }

private:
  void OnImageReady(View view)
  {
    ImageView iv = ImageView::DownCast(view);
    if(iv && iv.GetLoadingStatus() == Ui::Visual::ResourceStatus::LOADED)
    {
      // Image is ready to display
    }
  }

  ImageView mImageView;
};
```

For animated images, connect to `AnimationFinishedSignal()` to detect when playback completes:

```cpp
class MyController : public ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  void SetupAnimatedView()
  {
    mAnimView = AnimatedImageView::New("animation.gif");
    mAnimView.SetLoopCount(3);
    mAnimView.AnimationFinishedSignal().Connect(this, &MyController::OnAnimationFinished);
    mAnimView.Play();
  }

private:
  void OnAnimationFinished(View view)
  {
    // Animation completed all loops
  }

  AnimatedImageView mAnimView;
};
```

### Checking Loading Status

Query the current loading status:

```cpp
Ui::Visual::ResourceStatus status = imageView.GetLoadingStatus();
if(status == Ui::Visual::ResourceStatus::LOADING)
{
  // Still loading
}
else if(status == Ui::Visual::ResourceStatus::LOADED)
{
  // Ready
}
else if(status == Ui::Visual::ResourceStatus::FAILED)
{
  // Load failed
}
```

### Reloading Images

Force a reload of the current image:

```cpp
imageView.Reload();
```
